originalTitle: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
title: Happy News! Our Cartoonist Friend Biniam is Free!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends across Africa! I have some super happy news to share with you today. It's a story about a very special artist, a brave man, who is finally free! Let's clap our hands and cheer for our friend, Biniam Solomon!

Meet Our Friend Biniam

Imagine someone who loves to draw funny and clever pictures. That's Biniam Solomon! He is a cartoonist. He used to draw many, many pictures for newspapers and magazines in a country called Eritrea. Eritrea is a beautiful country in Africa, just like yours! Biniam's pictures were not just pretty; they were very smart. They made people laugh, but they also made people think about important things happening in their country. He was like a storyteller, but with a pen and paper instead of words. Did you know? Cartoons are special drawings that can tell a whole story or share a big idea without needing many words! Sometimes they are very funny, and sometimes they make you think.

What Does a Cartoonist Do?

A cartoonist is like a magic artist! They draw pictures that often have a message. Imagine drawing a lion to show strength, or a little bird to show freedom. Biniam used his drawings to talk about things that were good, and sometimes things that were not so good or fair. His cartoons were 'witty' and 'critical'. That means they were very clever and smart. They sometimes showed problems in a funny way, so people would notice and think about how to make things better. It's like when you draw a picture to show how you feel, or to show something important to you. Did you know? The word 'cartoon' comes from an old Italian word, 'cartone', which means a strong paper or cardboard. Cool, right?

Eritrea: A Special Country

Eritrea is a lovely country on the map of Africa. It has sunny skies and warm people, just like many places you know. Biniam lived and worked there, drawing his wonderful pictures for everyone to see and enjoy. He loved his country very much. For a long time, people in Eritrea could read Biniam's cartoons and other stories in their newspapers. This is called having a 'free press'. It means that newspapers and TV can share many different ideas and news without someone stopping them. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries! Each one is special and beautiful in its own way, like Eritrea.

When Things Changed

Sadly, many years ago, things changed in Eritrea. The free press stopped. This meant that newspapers and TV could not print or show all the stories and ideas they wanted. It became harder for people like Biniam to share their clever drawings and messages. Because Biniam drew pictures that made people think, and sometimes pointed out things that could be better, he was taken away. He was put in a place called prison. Prison is where people go if they have broken the rules or done something wrong. But here is the very sad part of the story: Biniam was held 'without charge'. This means no one ever told him what rule he had broken. No one ever said what he had done wrong. He never had a judge or a lawyer to help him.

A Long, Long Wait

Biniam stayed in prison for a very, very long time. Can you guess how long? He was there for 15 years! That's a super long time. If you are 7 years old, Biniam was in prison for more than twice your age! Imagine being away from your family and friends for that long. For 15 years, Biniam's family missed him. His friends missed him. And everyone who loved his clever cartoons missed his drawings. It must have been very hard and sad for them all. Did you know? Fifteen years is enough time for you to go to kindergarten, primary school, and then even high school! That's how long Biniam was away.

The Wonderful News!

But guess what? After all that time, something amazing happened! Biniam Solomon is finally free! Yes, you heard that right! He has been let out of prison and can go home. This is such happy news for Biniam, for his family, and for everyone who believes in freedom. He can finally hug his loved ones and see the world outside the prison walls again. This is a big reason to smile and celebrate!

Why is Freedom Important?

Being free is very important for everyone. It means you can play, learn, and express yourself. It means you can share your ideas and draw your pictures, like Biniam loves to do. It means you can be with your family and choose what you want to do. When people like Biniam are free, it reminds us that everyone deserves to be treated fairly. Everyone should have the right to speak up and share their thoughts, especially if they are doing it kindly and cleverly, just like Biniam did with his cartoons. Did you know? Being able to express your ideas, even through drawings, is a very important right for all people, all over the world!

What's Next for Biniam?

We hope that Biniam can now rest, be with his family, and maybe, just maybe, start drawing again. Imagine all the new, happy pictures he might draw now that he is free! We send him lots of love and good wishes from all of us. This story reminds us to always be kind, to speak up for what is fair, and to celebrate when good things happen, especially when someone gets their freedom back. Let's all cheer for Biniam Solomon!
summary: A beloved cartoonist from Eritrea, Biniam Solomon, has been set free after being held in prison for 15 years without anyone saying what he did wrong, bringing much happiness to his family and friends.
whyMatters: This story matters because Biniam is finally free after a very long time, showing us that freedom and being treated fairly are super important for everyone in the world.
